description:
You are a data-engineer who has just received a legacy customer extract that needs to be migrated to a newer “target” location while enforcing a very simple business rule: only adults (age ≥ 18) are allowed in the new file. Everything must be done from the Linux terminal inside /home/user. No GUI tools are available. INITIAL DATA • Path: /home/user/data/source/customers_legacy.csv • Encoding: UTF-8, Unix (LF) line endings. • Exact contents (including header line and the precise order of the columns): 
customer_id,name,age,signup_date
1,Alice,30,2021-01-05
2,Bob,17,2022-07-12
3,Charlie,25,2019-11-23
4,David,15,2020-02-14
5,Eva,40,2018-06-30
6,Frank,22,2023-03-01
WHAT YOU MUST PRODUCE 1. A new directory at /home/user/data/target. 2. Inside that directory, a file named customers_adults.csv that a. keeps the exact same header line as the source file; b. contains ONLY the rows whose age field is 18 or above; c. converts the content of the name column to upper-case letters; d. preserves the original column order and all other data unchanged. 3. Inside /home/user/data, create a plain-text log named migration_report.log with **exactly** three lines (no extra whitespace, no blank lines, Unix LF endings): 
TOTAL_SOURCE_ROWS: 6
TOTAL_TARGET_ROWS: 4
MIGRATION_STATUS: SUCCESS
CONSTRAINTS • The entire task can be completed with a handful of standard shell utilities (e.g., mkdir, cat, awk, cut, grep, etc.). • You do NOT need sudo or any privileged operations. • All paths must be created relative to the existing /home/user directory tree exactly as spelled above. • Make sure the two new files (customers_adults.csv and migration_report.log) are world-readable (default umask is fine). • Issue whatever commands you want, but at the end the automated grader will only inspect the filesystem and the log; console output does not matter. VERIFICATION HINT The grader will fail the exercise if • the log file path or name deviates, • any of the three log lines differ in spelling, order, or contain leading/trailing spaces, • the target CSV misses the header, has the wrong number of rows, or the name values are not all uppercase. Objective: perform the migration, build the validation log, and leave the system in the described final state.
truth:
Initial environment (already present when the agent starts):
1. Directory: /home/user/data/source
2. File: /home/user/data/source/customers_legacy.csv  
   Content (LF line endings):
customer_id,name,age,signup_date
1,Alice,30,2021-01-05
2,Bob,17,2022-07-12
3,Charlie,25,2019-11-23
4,David,15,2020-02-14
5,Eva,40,2018-06-30
6,Frank,22,2023-03-01


Expected artifacts after successful execution:

A. /home/user/data/target/customers_adults.csv (LF line endings)
customer_id,name,age,signup_date
1,ALICE,30,2021-01-05
3,CHARLIE,25,2019-11-23
5,EVA,40,2018-06-30
6,FRANK,22,2023-03-01

B. /home/user/data/migration_report.log (LF line endings)
TOTAL_SOURCE_ROWS: 6
TOTAL_TARGET_ROWS: 4
MIGRATION_STATUS: SUCCESS
name: task_000000_412c7af0
